revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Main Hall |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|kami|ja|神}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川 (三重県)}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川}}, {{ill|Ōdai, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮}}, it ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[ja:多岐原神社]][[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[sn:akihara Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[ja:多岐原神社]][[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[sn:akihara Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Main Hall |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|kami|ja|神}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川 (三重県)}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川}}, {{ill|Ōdai, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮}}, it ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[ja:多岐原神社]][[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[sn:akihara Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[ja:多岐原神社]][[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[sn:akihara Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{Shrine |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Main Hall |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|kami|ja|神}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川 (三重県)}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川}}, {{ill|Ōdai, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮}}, it ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[ja:多岐原神社]][[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[sn:akihara Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[ja:多岐原神社]][[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[sn:akihara Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Main Hall |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|kami|ja|神}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川 (三重県)}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川}}, {{ill|Ōdai, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮}}, it ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[ja:多岐原神社]][[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[Category:多岐原神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[sn:akihara Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takihara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Main Hall |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町|12=draft}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|kami|ja|神|12=draft}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川 (三重県)|12=draft}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|12=draft}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōdai, Mie|ja|大紀町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|12=draft}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|12=draft}}, it ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=draft}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|12=draft}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|12=draft}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|12=draft}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=draft}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯|12=draft}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|12=draft}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|12=draft}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]

{{Shrine |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Main Hall |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町|12=draft}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|kami|ja|神|12=draft}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川 (三重県)|12=draft}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|12=draft}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōdai, Mie|ja|大紀町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|12=draft}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|12=draft}}, it ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=draft}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|12=draft}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|12=draft}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|12=draft}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=draft}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯|12=draft}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|12=draft}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|12=draft}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]

The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=draft}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|12=draft}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|12=draft}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|12=draft}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=draft}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯|12=draft}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|12=draft}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|12=draft}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]

{{Shrine |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Main Hall |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町|12=draft}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|kami|ja|神|12=draft}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川 (三重県)|12=draft}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|12=draft}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōdai, Mie|ja|大紀町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|12=draft}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|12=draft}}, it ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=draft}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|12=draft}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|12=draft}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|12=draft}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=draft}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯|12=draft}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|12=draft}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|12=draft}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]
