The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|12=draft}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Main Hall |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|kami|ja|神|12=draft|12=draft}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川 (三重県)|12=draft|12=draft}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōdai, Mie|ja|大紀町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, it ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|12=draft|12=draft}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|12=draft|12=draft}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|12=draft|12=draft}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft|12=draft}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯|12=draft|12=draft}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|12=draft|12=draft}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft|12=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|12=draft|12=draft}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]

Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Refreshing imported pages]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Main Hall |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|kami|ja|神|12=draft|12=draft}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川 (三重県)|12=draft|12=draft}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōdai, Mie|ja|大紀町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, it Ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|12=draft|12=draft}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|12=draft|12=draft}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|12=draft|12=draft}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft|12=draft}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯|12=draft|12=draft}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|12=draft|12=draft}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft|12=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|12=draft|12=draft}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Engishiki Jinmyōchō|" to "Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|"
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Main Hall |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|kami|ja|神|12=draft|12=draft}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川 (三重県)|12=draft|12=draft}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōdai, Mie|ja|大紀町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, it Ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|12=draft|12=draft}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|12=draft|12=draft}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|12=draft|12=draft}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft|12=draft}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯|12=draft|12=draft}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|12=draft|12=draft}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft|12=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|12=draft|12=draft}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:摂末社]] to [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Main Hall |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|kami|ja|神|12=draft|12=draft}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川 (三重県)|12=draft|12=draft}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōdai, Mie|ja|大紀町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, it Ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|12=draft|12=draft}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|12=draft|12=draft}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|12=draft|12=draft}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft|12=draft}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯|12=draft|12=draft}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|12=draft|12=draft}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft|12=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|12=draft|12=draft}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|12=draft" to "|qq=draft"
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Main Hall |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|kami|ja|神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川 (三重県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōdai, Mie|ja|大紀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it Ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|qq=draft|qq=draft}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]

revision.comment: 10 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:多岐原神社 up to rev 94421507
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 多岐原神社 |画像=[[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と社殿 |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]三瀬川[[小字|字]]久保海道94 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神= 真奈胡神 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
[[皇大神宮]]摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= まなごさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=多岐原神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''多岐原神社'''（たきはらじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。土地の[[神 (神道)|神]]が[[倭姫命]]を助けたことから、倭姫命が定め祀った[[神社]]である伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：84ページ学研パブリッシング（2013）：73ページ。 [[宮川 (三重県)|宮川]]沿いに鎮座し、[[境内]]の木立の中に川のせせらぎが響く。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]三瀬川に鎮座する式内社研究会 編（1990）：227ページ。内宮別宮の[[瀧原宮]]から宮川を約6[[キロメートル|km]]下ったところにある。内宮の摂社27社のうち、第27位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。伊勢神宮の摂社・末社・所管社には通例、[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない伊勢文化舎（2008）：26ページが、多岐原神社には賽銭箱が置かれている。 [[社殿]]は[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]で、南向きに建つ式内社研究会 編（1990）：228ページ。一重の[[玉垣]]が社殿を囲み、[[神明鳥居]]1基を有する。社地の面積は5反9畝12歩（≒5,890.9[[平方メートル|m2]]）宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：11ページ。 [[祭神]]は、真奈胡神（まなごのかみ、まなこのかみ）。倭姫命が急流を渡れずに困っていたところを真奈胡神が助けたとされる。中川経雅は祭神名「まなこ」を「眞之子」、すなわち瀧原宮の祭神の子であるという解釈を行ったが、真相は不明である。 == 社名 == 『皇太神宮儀式帳』では「瀧原」と表記し、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では[[写本]]によって「多伎原」・「多紀原」と表記が異なるが、後代に「多岐原」に統一された。『倭姫命世記』は、「御瀬社」と記載する。 『大同元年神宮本記』には「眞奈胡神社」とあり、地域住民からは「まなごさん」と呼ばれ親しまれ、遠くへ移ってしまった[[氏神]]の代わりとした[[祭礼]]も捧げられる櫻井（1991）：293 - 294ページ。 == 歴史 == 『倭姫命世記』などに記載された伝承によれば、[[アマテラス|天照大神]]を奉じて巡幸中の倭姫命が宮川を渡ろうとするも、流れが速く困っていると、真奈胡神は渡れるところを案内し、その縁で多岐原神社が定められたとされる式内社研究会 編（1990）：227 - 228ページ。確実なところでは、伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より『延喜式神名帳』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に創建された伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：22ページ。『皇大神宮儀式帳』にも記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在したことになる。他の神宮摂末社と同様、[[祝部]]と呼ばれる日常の祭祀役が任命されていたようであり、『眞奈胡由来記』で祝部の存在が確認できる。 [[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に大宮司・[[河辺精長|河邊精長]]（大中臣精長）によって再興された。再興された社地が荒廃前の旧社地であるかは明確ではないが、立地場所の環境と倭姫命の伝説を照らし合わせて、おおむね同じ場所であると見ることができる。[[享保]]9年（[[1724年]]）、[[紀州藩]]は「殺生禁断」の石標を建て、享保17年（[[1732年]]）には三瀬川村が[[常夜灯]]を2基献灯した。 [[1904年]]（明治37年）10月に社殿の建て替えが行われた。 == 三瀬坂峠と三瀬の渡し跡 == 多岐原神社と瀧原宮を結ぶ[[熊野街道]][[伊勢路 (熊野古道)|伊勢路]]は[[熊野三山]]へ向かう[[巡礼]]路の一部であった伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：84, 86ページ。両神社の間には三瀬坂峠（みせざかとうげ）があり、急勾配区間となっている。三瀬坂峠は、[[世界文化遺産]]『[[紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]]』の構成遺産からは外れているが、茶屋跡や宝暦地蔵などが残っている伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：86ページ。多岐原神社から瀧原宮までは、三瀬坂峠を越えて徒歩約1時間17分（約4.1km）である伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。 多岐原神社から150[[メートル|m]]ほど進むと、「三瀬の渡し」の跡がある。これも熊野街道伊勢路の一部であった。三瀬の渡し周辺は[[戦後]]の河川改修により、往時と[[景観]]が変わっている。 == 祭祀 == [[祈年祭]]（[[2月19日]]）、[[月次祭]]（[[6月22日]]・[[12月22日]]）、[[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]）が境内にて奉仕される。 鎮座地の三瀬川地区は、[[1908年]]（明治41年）に氏神が40町（≒4.36km）離れた地に[[合祀]]されてしまった櫻井（1991）：293ページ。このため、氏神で行っていた[[庚申待|おこもり]]を[[6月19日]]に多岐原神社で行うようになり、2月9日には同氏神で行っていた「御鍬神事」の代わりに[[餅まき|餅投げ]]を多岐原神社社頭で行っている。 == 交通 == ;公共交通 :[[多気郡]][[大台町]]にある最寄駅の[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]][[三瀬谷駅]]からは3.6km離れており、徒歩で約1時間8分かかる。神社の近くに案内板がある。 ;自家用車 :[[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]より[[三重県道747号打見大台線]]経由、約4分（約3.4km）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:たきはらしんしや}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[多岐原神社]] to [[Takihara Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 多岐原神社 |画像=[[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と社殿 |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]三瀬川[[小字|字]]久保海道94 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神= 真奈胡神 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
[[皇大神宮]]摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= まなごさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=多岐原神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''多岐原神社'''（たきはらじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。土地の[[神 (神道)|神]]が[[倭姫命]]を助けたことから、倭姫命が定め祀った[[神社]]である伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：84ページ学研パブリッシング（2013）：73ページ。 [[宮川 (三重県)|宮川]]沿いに鎮座し、[[境内]]の木立の中に川のせせらぎが響く。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]三瀬川に鎮座する式内社研究会 編（1990）：227ページ。内宮別宮の[[瀧原宮]]から宮川を約6[[キロメートル|km]]下ったところにある。内宮の摂社27社のうち、第27位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。伊勢神宮の摂社・末社・所管社には通例、[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない伊勢文化舎（2008）：26ページが、多岐原神社には賽銭箱が置かれている。 [[社殿]]は[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]で、南向きに建つ式内社研究会 編（1990）：228ページ。一重の[[玉垣]]が社殿を囲み、[[神明鳥居]]1基を有する。社地の面積は5反9畝12歩（≒5,890.9[[平方メートル|m2]]）宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：11ページ。 [[祭神]]は、真奈胡神（まなごのかみ、まなこのかみ）。倭姫命が急流を渡れずに困っていたところを真奈胡神が助けたとされる。中川経雅は祭神名「まなこ」を「眞之子」、すなわち瀧原宮の祭神の子であるという解釈を行ったが、真相は不明である。 == 社名 == 『皇太神宮儀式帳』では「瀧原」と表記し、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では[[写本]]によって「多伎原」・「多紀原」と表記が異なるが、後代に「多岐原」に統一された。『倭姫命世記』は、「御瀬社」と記載する。 『大同元年神宮本記』には「眞奈胡神社」とあり、地域住民からは「まなごさん」と呼ばれ親しまれ、遠くへ移ってしまった[[氏神]]の代わりとした[[祭礼]]も捧げられる櫻井（1991）：293 - 294ページ。 == 歴史 == 『倭姫命世記』などに記載された伝承によれば、[[アマテラス|天照大神]]を奉じて巡幸中の倭姫命が宮川を渡ろうとするも、流れが速く困っていると、真奈胡神は渡れるところを案内し、その縁で多岐原神社が定められたとされる式内社研究会 編（1990）：227 - 228ページ。確実なところでは、伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より『延喜式神名帳』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に創建された伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：22ページ。『皇大神宮儀式帳』にも記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在したことになる。他の神宮摂末社と同様、[[祝部]]と呼ばれる日常の祭祀役が任命されていたようであり、『眞奈胡由来記』で祝部の存在が確認できる。 [[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に大宮司・[[河辺精長|河邊精長]]（大中臣精長）によって再興された。再興された社地が荒廃前の旧社地であるかは明確ではないが、立地場所の環境と倭姫命の伝説を照らし合わせて、おおむね同じ場所であると見ることができる。[[享保]]9年（[[1724年]]）、[[紀州藩]]は「殺生禁断」の石標を建て、享保17年（[[1732年]]）には三瀬川村が[[常夜灯]]を2基献灯した。 [[1904年]]（明治37年）10月に社殿の建て替えが行われた。 == 三瀬坂峠と三瀬の渡し跡 == 多岐原神社と瀧原宮を結ぶ[[熊野街道]][[伊勢路 (熊野古道)|伊勢路]]は[[熊野三山]]へ向かう[[巡礼]]路の一部であった伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：84, 86ページ。両神社の間には三瀬坂峠（みせざかとうげ）があり、急勾配区間となっている。三瀬坂峠は、[[世界文化遺産]]『[[紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]]』の構成遺産からは外れているが、茶屋跡や宝暦地蔵などが残っている伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：86ページ。多岐原神社から瀧原宮までは、三瀬坂峠を越えて徒歩約1時間17分（約4.1km）である伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。 多岐原神社から150[[メートル|m]]ほど進むと、「三瀬の渡し」の跡がある。これも熊野街道伊勢路の一部であった。三瀬の渡し周辺は[[戦後]]の河川改修により、往時と[[景観]]が変わっている。 == 祭祀 == [[祈年祭]]（[[2月19日]]）、[[月次祭]]（[[6月22日]]・[[12月22日]]）、[[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]）が境内にて奉仕される。 鎮座地の三瀬川地区は、[[1908年]]（明治41年）に氏神が40町（≒4.36km）離れた地に[[合祀]]されてしまった櫻井（1991）：293ページ。このため、氏神で行っていた[[庚申待|おこもり]]を[[6月19日]]に多岐原神社で行うようになり、2月9日には同氏神で行っていた「御鍬神事」の代わりに[[餅まき|餅投げ]]を多岐原神社社頭で行っている。 == 交通 == ;公共交通 :[[多気郡]][[大台町]]にある最寄駅の[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]][[三瀬谷駅]]からは3.6km離れており、徒歩で約1時間8分かかる。神社の近くに案内板がある。 ;自家用車 :[[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]より[[三重県道747号打見大台線]]経由、約4分（約3.4km）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:たきはらしんしや}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Main Hall |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|kami|ja|神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川 (三重県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōdai, Mie|ja|大紀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it Ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|qq=draft|qq=draft}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|多岐原神社|version=94421507|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Bot: cleanup/reformat
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takihara-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Main Hall |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|kami|ja|神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川 (三重県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōdai, Mie|ja|大紀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it Ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|qq=draft|qq=draft}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Chōchin|ja|常夜灯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|ja|三瀬谷駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|ja|紀勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|多岐原神社|version=94421507|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:多岐原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[sn:Takihara Shrine]] [[sn:akihara Shrine]]
