{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Takihara-jinja.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|en|Taiki, Mie|WD=Q1203189|lt=Taiki, Mie}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.394722|N|136.430167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Taiki|WD=Q1203189}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=kami}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River (Mie)}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara Shrine|qq=}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misedagawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|en|Taiki, Mie|WD=Q1203189|lt=Ōdai, Mie}}, [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa|en|Miyagawa|WD=Q1665818|lt=Miyagawa River (Mie)}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|1=Naikū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23996212}}, it Ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|en|Misedani Station|WD=Q5357769|lt=Misedani Station}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 747|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 747|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358134}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|1=Omiya-Odai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10934923}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|en|Kisei Expressway|WD=Q6416449|lt=Kisei Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|多岐原神社|version=94421507|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Takihara Shrine (Q17229062)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Takihara-jinja.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|en|Taiki, Mie|WD=Q1203189|lt=Taiki, Mie}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.394722|N|136.430167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Taiki|WD=Q1203189}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=kami}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River (Mie)}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara Shrine|qq=}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misedagawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|en|Taiki, Mie|WD=Q1203189|lt=Ōdai, Mie}}, [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa|en|Miyagawa|WD=Q1665818|lt=Miyagawa River (Mie)}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|1=Naikū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23996212}}, it Ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manago no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manago-san|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishu Domain}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Nightlight|en|Nightlight|WD=Q762277|lt=Chōchin}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iseji Route|1=Iseji Route|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379139}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodo}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|en|Misedani Station|WD=Q5357769|lt=Misedani Station}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 747|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 747|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358134}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|1=Omiya-Odai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10934923}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|en|Kisei Expressway|WD=Q6416449|lt=Kisei Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|多岐原神社|version=94421507|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Takihara Shrine (Q17229062)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Takihara Shrine|lt=Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|WD=Q17229062}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} || — || {{coord|34.395008|136.430461|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181123 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17229062}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17229062]] [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings in Oki Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Taiki,_Mie]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Takihara-jinja.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|en|Taiki, Mie|WD=Q1203189|lt=Taiki, Mie}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.394722|N|136.430167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Taiki|WD=Q1203189}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=kami}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River (Mie)}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara Shrine|qq=}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misedagawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|en|Taiki, Mie|WD=Q1203189|lt=Ōdai, Mie}}, [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa|en|Miyagawa|WD=Q1665818|lt=Miyagawa River (Mie)}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|1=Naikū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23996212}}, it Ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manago no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manago-san|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishu Domain}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Nightlight|en|Nightlight|WD=Q762277|lt=Chōchin}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iseji Route|1=Iseji Route|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379139}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodo}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|en|Misedani Station|WD=Q5357769|lt=Misedani Station}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 747|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 747|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358134}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|1=Omiya-Odai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10934923}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|en|Kisei Expressway|WD=Q6416449|lt=Kisei Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|多岐原神社|version=94421507|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Takihara Shrine (Q17229062)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Takihara Shrine|lt=Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|WD=Q17229062}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} || — || {{coord|34.395008|136.430461|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181123 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17229062}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17229062]] [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings in Oki Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Taiki,_Mie]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manago-san|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishu Domain}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Nightlight|en|Nightlight|WD=Q762277|lt=Chōchin}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iseji Route|1=Iseji Route|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379139}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodo}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|en|Misedani Station|WD=Q5357769|lt=Misedani Station}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 747|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 747|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358134}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|1=Omiya-Odai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10934923}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|en|Kisei Expressway|WD=Q6416449|lt=Kisei Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|多岐原神社|version=94421507|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Takihara Shrine (Q17229062)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Takihara Shrine|lt=Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|WD=Q17229062}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} || — || {{coord|34.395008|136.430461|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181123 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17229062}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17229062]] [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings in Oki Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Taiki,_Mie]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Takihara-jinja.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|en|Taiki, Mie|WD=Q1203189|lt=Taiki, Mie}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.394722|N|136.430167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Taiki|WD=Q1203189}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=kami}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River (Mie)}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara Shrine|qq=}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misedagawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|en|Taiki, Mie|WD=Q1203189|lt=Ōdai, Mie}}, [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa|en|Miyagawa|WD=Q1665818|lt=Miyagawa River (Mie)}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|1=Naikū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23996212}}, it Ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manago no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manago-san|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishu Domain}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Nightlight|en|Nightlight|WD=Q762277|lt=Chōchin}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iseji Route|1=Iseji Route|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379139}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodo}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|en|Misedani Station|WD=Q5357769|lt=Misedani Station}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 747|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 747|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358134}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|1=Omiya-Odai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10934923}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|en|Kisei Expressway|WD=Q6416449|lt=Kisei Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Takihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Takihara-jinja.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|en|Taiki, Mie|WD=Q1203189|lt=Taiki, Mie}}, Misekawa, Kubo Kaido 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.394722|N|136.430167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Manago no Kami |type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other Names = Manago-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} (October 22) |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41|lon_deg=136|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.6|label=Takihara Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|41|N|136|25|48.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Taiki|WD=Q1203189}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Takihara Shrine''' (多岐原神社, *Takihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It was established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, who enshrined the local {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=kami}} in gratitude for its assistanceIse Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 84Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The shrine is located along the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River (Mie)}} with the gentle murmur of the river resonating through the trees of the precincts. == Overview == {{ill|Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara Shrine|qq=}} is located in {{ill|Misedagawa|ja|三瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misedagawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Taiki, Mie|en|Taiki, Mie|WD=Q1203189|lt=Ōdai, Mie}}, [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 227. It lies approximately 6 km downstream along the {{ill|Miyagawa|en|Miyagawa|WD=Q1665818|lt=Miyagawa River (Mie)}} from {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū (Ise Grand Shrine)|1=Naikū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23996212}}, it Ranks 27thUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11. Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manago no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manago-san|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishu Domain}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Nightlight|en|Nightlight|WD=Q762277|lt=Chōchin}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iseji Route|1=Iseji Route|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379139}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodo}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|en|Misedani Station|WD=Q5357769|lt=Misedani Station}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 747|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 747|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358134}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|1=Omiya-Odai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10934923}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|en|Kisei Expressway|WD=Q6416449|lt=Kisei Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|多岐原神社|version=94421507|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Takihara Shrine (Q17229062)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Takihara Shrine|lt=Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|WD=Q17229062}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} || — || {{coord|34.395008|136.430461|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181123 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17229062}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17229062]] [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings in Oki Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Taiki,_Mie]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

Unlike most auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, which typically do not have offering boxesIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 26, Takihara Shrine does have one. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} architectural style with a wooden plank roof, facing southShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 228. The shrine precinct is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fencing, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=Torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 5,890.9 square metersUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Manago no Kami|ja|真奈胡神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manago no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (pronounced "manago no kami" or "manako no kami"). According to legend, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was struggling to cross a rapid current when Manago no Kami assisted her. Although scholar Nakagawa Tsunemasa interpreted the name "Manako" as "眞之子" ("true child"), implying that this deity might be the offspring of the deity enshrined at Takihara-no-Miya, the true origin remains unclear. == Shrine Name == In historical records, the shrine is referred to with various kanji, including "瀧原" in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja|皇太神宮儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}} and "多伎原" or "多紀原" in different manuscripts of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The current standard form "多岐原" was adopted in later periods. In the {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, it is listed as "御瀬社". Locally, it is affectionately called "{{ill|Manago-san|ja|まなごさん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manago-san|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}", and serves as a substitute for ancestral shrine rituals following the relocation of the original {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami|1=Ujigami}}Sakurai (1991): pp. 293-294. == History == According to legends recorded in texts such as the Yamato-hime-no-mikoto-seiki, Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, while carrying the sacred spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, attempted to cross the fast-flowing Miyagawa River. Manago no Kami guided her across, and the shrine was established in commemoration of this eventShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 227-228. Historically, the shrine was founded before the compilation of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō in 927 ADIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō, indicating its existence before 804 AD. In 1663, the shrine was restored by Ōmiya-shi {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). Though it is unclear whether the current site is the original location, geographical features and legends suggest continuity. In 1724, the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishu Domain|1=Kishū Domain}} erected a "prohibition of killing" stone marker, and in 1732, two {{ill|Nightlight|en|Nightlight|WD=Q762277|lt=Chōchin|1=Nightlight}} lanterns were donated by the village of Misekawa. The shrine building was reconstructed in October 1904. == Mitsesaka Pass and Mitsese Crossing Site == The route connecting Takihara Shrine and Takihara-no-Miya is part of the {{ill|Iseji Route|ja|伊勢路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iseji Route|1=Iseji Route|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379139}} of the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodo|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a pilgrimage path leading to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): pp. 84, 86. The path includes the Mitsesaka Pass, characterized by steep inclines. Though not part of the UNESCO World Heritage "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," remnants such as old teahouse sites and the Hōreki Jizō statue remainIse Bunkasha (2008): p. 86. About 150 meters from Takihara Shrine lies the site of the "Mitsese Crossing," once part of the Iseji Route. Post-war river improvements have altered the landscape. == Rituals == The shrine hosts the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai|1=Kinensai}} on February 19, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} on June 22 and December 22, and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} on October 22. After the local ujigami was merged with another shrine 4.36 km away in 1908, the community began holding their {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}} ritual at Takihara Shrine on June 19, and a rice-cake throwing event on February 9 as a substitute for the former "Mikuwajinji" ritual. == Access == ; Public Transport Takihara Shrine is approximately 3.6 km from {{ill|Misedani Station|en|Misedani Station|WD=Q5357769|lt=Misedani Station|1=Misedani Station}} on the JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line|1=Kisei Main Line}}, about a 1 hour and 8-minute walk. Signboards are posted nearby. ; By Car About 4 minutes (3.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 747|ja|三重県道747号打見大台線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 747|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 747|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358134}} from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|1=Omiya-Odai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10934923}} on the {{ill|Kisei Expressway|en|Kisei Expressway|WD=Q6416449|lt=Kisei Expressway|1=Kisei Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_03.jpg 多岐原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1301.html 多岐原神社（たきはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|多岐原神社|version=94421507|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Takihara Shrine (Q17229062)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Takihara Shrine|lt=Takihara Shrine|ja|多岐原神社|WD=Q17229062|1=Takihara Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || — || {{coord|34.395008|136.430461|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181123 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17229062}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17229062]] [[ja:多岐原神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings in Oki Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Taiki,_Mie]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]
